title: Asset Allocation Insights - March 2026
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: In March 2026, equity overweight remains due to AI-driven growth, while bonds are underweighted. U.S. equities are balanced with real estate adjustments. Portfo
excerpt: In March 2026, equity overweight remains due to AI-driven growth, while bonds are underweighted. U.S. equities are balanced with real estate adjustments. Portfolios favor cyclical momentum and risk assets.

content.introduction: In March 2026, equity overweight remains due to AI-driven growth, while bonds are underweighted. U.S. equities are balanced with real estate adjustments. Portfolios favor cyclical momentum and risk assets.
body: - Global tech firms are investing over $600B in AI-driven infrastructure, boosting growth and validating an equity overweight. - Easing financial conditions and fiscal stimulus support a cyclical upswing, favoring risk assets over bonds amid structural growth. - Discipline in asset allocation prioritizes non-US equities and commodities, balancing cyclical momentum with valuation realism. We are in a genuine cyclical upswing, supported by converging forces: robust growth across major economies, easing financial conditions, and a historic wave of AI-driven capital expenditure. For portfolio construction, this matters: it validates an overweight to equities and a corresponding underweight to bonds, as the risk-adjusted return profile shifts toward growth-sensitive instruments. ## Equity Allocation: Overweight Retained, But Selectivity Is Key. We maintain a modest overweight to risk assets, including equities, and have added to existing positions over the past month. The fundamental case remains intact: a historic wave of AI-driven capital expenditure-over USD 600 billion this year-provides a structural tailwind that transcends typical cyclical swings.

# March 2026 Asset Allocation Update For Risk-Managed Portfolios. March 2026 asset allocation changes grid for Blueprint Investment Partners risk-managed global portfolios. What's illustrated above most closely reflects allocation adjustments for the Growth Strategy. Diversification does not guarantee investment returns and does not eliminate the risk of loss. #### U.S. Equities. Both the intermediate- and long-term trends are positive, but a portion of the current overweight allocation will be returned to strengthening real estate securities. Exposure will not change, with both foreign developed and emerging market equities remaining at their baseline allocation. Exposure will increase to baseline. Trends are now positive across all timeframes and the asset class has strengthened relative to U.S. equities as well. #### U.S. Both U.S. and international Treasuries are now experiencing some positive trends and strengthening. Outside of fixed income, the most influential segments continue to be net long equities followed by international currencies and precious metals. Exposure will decrease to minimum as exposure is returned to strengthening U.S. and international Treasuries.
